frameworks.name: Bayesian learning for singular learning machines
frameworks.one_line: posterior inference over models with non-identifiable / degenerate parameter structure

frameworks.name: exchange Monte Carlo (parallel tempering)
frameworks.one_line: replica-exchange MCMC variant applied to singular posteriors

frameworks.name: Markov chain Monte Carlo (Metropolis)
frameworks.one_line: conventional MCMC baseline that struggles with singularities

frameworks.name: maximum-likelihood estimation
frameworks.one_line: point-estimation baseline outperformed by Bayesian learning here

core_question: Does exchange Monte Carlo sampling improve Bayesian posterior estimation more for singular learning machines than for regular ones?
key_assumptions: neural nets, mixtures, Bayesian networks, HMMs share singular parameter geometry
Bayesian learning outperforms ML estimation for these models
stochastic complexity has a known theoretical value to compare against
manipulations: sampler choice (exchange MC vs conventional MCMC)
model class (singular vs regular learning machines)
key_findings: exchange MC yields better Bayesian posterior sampling on singular machines than on regular ones
numerical stochastic complexity from exchange MC tracks the theoretical value
interpretations: singularity structure is precisely where exchange MC's tempering benefit is largest
exchange MC is an effective practical tool for Bayesian inference in singular models
